title: carex phalaroides subsp. crassiflora
intro: Carex phalaroides subsp. crassiflora, also known as the rough-stemmed sedge, is a plant species in the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and reducing inflammation. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas and infusions, which are believed to provide health benefits such as relieving digestive discomfort and reducing fever. In traditional medicine, Carex phalaroides subsp. crassiflora is often prepared as a decoction or tincture to utilize its medicinal properties.
